## [0.2.0](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/compare/code-review-action@v0.1.0...code-review-action@v0.2.0) (2026-05-29)

## Release Notes

Code review bot now smartly handles acknowledgements and won't miss rapid-fire mentions.

## ✨ What's New

### Automatic release merging
Release pull requests that are approved and pass all checks now merge automatically, eliminating the manual merge step before publishing. The new release-automerge action integrates seamlessly with your existing release pipeline.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#107: Add release-automerge composite action with downstream sync workflow](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/107)
</details>

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

### Smarter acknowledgement handling
The AI reviewer now recognizes when you're simply acknowledging its feedback (like "Fixed" or "Done") versus asking a question. For simple acknowledgements, it adds a 👍 reaction instead of generating unnecessary reply comments, while still responding helpfully to questions or re-review requests.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#111: Code review react mode replies to every review-thread acknowledgement](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/111)
</details>

### Reliable mention processing
The bot no longer ignores @-mentions when multiple comments arrive in quick succession. Each comment now gets its own processing queue, ensuring every mention triggers a response regardless of timing.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#71: Code review action drops bot mentions when comments arrive in quick succession](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/71)
</details>
